# spec-code-coupling-check bypass list (Cut D W-08; spec 127 follow-up).
#
# One prefix per line. Trailing `/` matches the prefix as a directory;
# no trailing `/` matches the path exactly. Lines starting with `#` are
# comments; blank lines are ignored.
#
# Pre-W-08 this list lived as `BYPASS_PREFIXES` inside the gate source.
# Externalising it makes the surface visible to reviewers in PR diffs
# and easier to audit. Adding an entry here is the equivalent of
# adding a coupling-rule exemption — every entry needs an owner in
# the file's git blame.
#
# Notably **NOT** bypassed:
#   - `Makefile`       — claimed by specs 104, 105, 116, 127.
#   - `AGENTS.md`      — claimed by spec 103.
#   - `crates/`, `tools/`, `apps/`, `packages/`, `platform/services/`
#                      — first-class code paths.

# Orthogonal scaffolding: CI metadata governed by spec 118's `# Spec:`
# header convention, not by this diff-based gate.
.github/

# Documentation tree.
docs/

# Root-level repo metadata files.
README.md
CLAUDE.md
DEVELOPERS.md
LICENSE
CHANGELOG.md
CODEOWNERS
.gitignore
.gitattributes
